content.description:
Sortable lists provide drag-to-reorder functionality for item attachments displayed in a linear list (as opposed to the spatial canvas layout).

## Concept

While the [spatial windowing system](item://ef793c27-2d4b-4c99-b05a-2769db5bc5a9) arranges children on a 2D canvas, some contexts benefit from an ordered list:

- Todo lists where order represents priority
- Document outlines where order represents sequence
- Playlists, reading lists, step-by-step instructions

Sortable lists render attachments in array order with drag handles for reordering.

## Implementation

The reorder operation modifies the parent item's `attachments` array:

1. User drags an item to a new position
2. The attachment is removed from its current index
3. Inserted at the target index
4. Parent item saved with `api.update()` to reflect new order

The `attachments` array is ordered — array position determines display order.

## Drag Interaction

- Drag handle (grip icon) on each list item
- Visual feedback: dragged item lifts, gap appears at drop target
- Drop saves the new order immediately
- Uses [silent updates](item://c0c0c0c0-0010-0000-0000-000000000000) during drag for smooth interaction

## See Also

- [Spatial Windowing](item://ef793c27-2d4b-4c99-b05a-2769db5bc5a9) — The 2D alternative to linear lists
- [Attachments](item://c0c0c0c0-0005-0000-0000-000000000000) — Ordered attachment arrays
